{{Quote|We're with you, Ludo Kressh.|Dor Gal-ram, during the Battle of Khar Delba|<ref name="flight" />}}
'''Dor Gal-ram''' was a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], [[5000 BBY/Legends|5,000 years before]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. A member of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]], Gal-ram was native to an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]], Gal-ram sided with one of the contenders for the title, [[Ludo Kressh]]. Kressh's main rival was [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], a progressive [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] with expansionist ambitions. In 5,000 BBY, Sadow staged an attack on the Sith Empire, meant to appear like a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] assault, and used this opportunity to declare himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. This led to a schism between Sadow and Kressh, with Gal-ram siding with Kressh. Sadow then staged another raid, this time leaving evidence that he was actually complicit; upon seeing this evidence, an angered Kressh ordered Gal-ram and a fellow supporter, [[Horak-mul]], to gather their forces to attack Sadow. In the ensuing [[Battle of Khar Delba|clash]] at [[Khar Delba]], Gal-ram and his allies were outwitted and defeated by Sadow. Gal-ram, along with Horak-mul, was killed in the battle when Sadow ordered the crew of Gal-ram's [[Dor Gal-ram's flagship|flagship]] to betray and kill him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|We are taking a great risk to be seen here.|Dor Gal-ram|<ref name="pawns">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:Dor_Gal-ram.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Gal-ram wearing his battle helmet]]
Dor Gal-ram was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] in the years leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. He was native to a region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] known as the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]].<ref name="flight" /> Sometime during his life, Gal-ram created a number of [[Dor Gal-ram and Horak-mul's tablets|tablets]] alongside fellow [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Horak-mul]].<ref name="Horak-Mul">{{TORcite|type=message|Horak-Mul|Engineer Sorrel}}</ref> In 5,000 BBY, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Marka Ragnos]] [[Death/Legends|died]] after a century of iron-handed rule. After Ragnos' death, two Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lords]], [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]], stepped in to try and fill the power vacuum.<ref name="funeral">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]</ref> Gal-ram supported Kressh, the more conservative Sith Lord, over Sadow, a progressive with expansionist ambitions.<ref name="pawns" /> Around this time, the arrival of two [[Human/Legends|Humans]] in the Sith Empire from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], opened up a conflict between Sadow and Kressh, who disagreed on how to deal with the Daragons.<ref name="funeral" />

Finally, Sadow staged an attack on the Sith Empire, which was made to look like a Republic assault, riling the Sith into a frenzy. During a meeting on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] after the raid, Sadow declared himself the new Dark Lord of the Sith. Gal-ram watched on in silence as the majority of other Sith Lords voiced their support of Sadow. An outraged Kressh called his remaining supporters to rally around him, and Gal-ram left the meeting with the Sith Lord.<ref name="fabric">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 3]]</ref> After the meeting, Sadow staged another false Republic attack on a [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] on Ziost, which contained the Daragons' [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]'', and stole the vessel. Upon arriving at the hangar, Kressh, found Sadow's emblem amidst the carnage. Shocked and angry, Kressh summoned Gal-ram and another of his supporters, the one-eyed Sith Lord Horak-mul, to his chambers on [[Rhelg/Legends|Rhelg]] to confer on these developments. There, Kressh informed them of Sadow's complicity in the attack and ordered Gal-ram and Horak-mul to gather their [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] so as to stage a [[Battle of Khar Delba|surprise attack]] on Sadow.<ref name="flight" />

Gal-ram did as he was told, massing his forces and joining Kressh's and Horak-mul's fleets at [[Khar Delba]], the site of [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|Sadow's citadel]].<ref name="pawns" /> They commenced their attack, launching a rain of [[Laser/Legends|laser]] fire onto Sadow's base. However, Sadow had prepared for the battle and had hidden his fleet in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Khar Delba's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Khar Shian]]. Sadow's fleet attacked and caught Gal-ram and his allies off-guard, and Sadow then moved on to the next stage of his plan: ordering the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] crew of [[Dor Gal-ram's flagship|Gal-ram's flagship]]<ref name="flight" />—who were, in reality, [[Sadow'een]], assassins under Sadow's command<ref name="Bitter Rivalries">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Bitter Rivalries|Hoth}}</ref>—to kill him. Gal-ram was preoccupied with the battle and the Massassi crew caught him unawares, murdering him. Horak-mul was killed in the same manner, and Kressh was defeated in the Battle of Khar Delba.<ref name="flight" />

==Personality and traits==
Dor Gal-ram was a staunch supporter of Ludo Kressh over Naga Sadow, but, knowing the dangers of speaking out against the new Dark Lord of the Sith, he kept his alignment secret. Gal-ram feared that meeting with Kressh on Rhelg following Sadow's theft of the Daragons' starship was an unnecessary risk, but he still rallied with Kressh against Sadow.<ref name="pawns" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dor Gal-ram first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, which was written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. Gal-ram first appeared in a non-speaking role in the arc's third issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|''The Fabric of an Empire'']]. His role would later be expanded in the fourth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Pawns of a Sith Lord'']], and the fifth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''The Flight of Starbreaker 12'']]. in which his character was killed. In these appearances, Gal-ram was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] In [[2008]], Gal-ram was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
